tick_over_tick_delta_vs_1100.interpretation: POWERGRID BUY 150 @ ₹320.50 (₹48,075 = 4.81% of capital) submitted as OPEN at 11:13:02 — the 3rd Power-sector name and 4th BUY of the session. This single submission satisfies the firing conditions of TWO pre-armed triggers from the 11:00 tick: (i) Trigger I (3rd Power name + Power exposure > 13%) — Power on full fill jumps from 9.92% to 14.73%, a clean 1.73 pp breach of the 13% trigger line; (ii) Trigger F reconfigured (4th BUY by 11:30 AND deployment > 65%) — both legs now fire with 4 BUYs (TATAPOWER 10:13, INDUSINDBK 10:42, HINDZINC 10:42, POWERGRID 11:13) and full-fill deployment 68.30% > 65% threshold. This is the strongest re-deployment cadence of the 4-day experiment: post-AUBANK exit at 09:31, ₹190,995 has been committed across 4 BUYs in 102 minutes, against only 1 SELL — the inverse of the system_prompt rule 8 SELL-before-BUY discipline at high deployment. Strategy has reverted toward the Day-1 binge-buy pattern that originally required the 0.75/0.70 deployed cap interventions.

findings: POWERGRID BUY (150 @ ₹320.50 = 4.81% of capital) submitted OPEN at 11:13:02. Third Power-sector name (joining filled NTPC 5.05% + still-OPEN TATAPOWER 4.87%). Power-sector exposure on full fill now 14.73% — a clean 1.73 pp breach of the 13% Trigger I line.

Trigger I (Power-cluster re-emergence, CARRIED) FIRES cleanly: 3rd Power name + 14.73% > 13% threshold. Pattern echoes the Day-1 Power cluster (TORNTPOWER + NTPC + ADANIPOWER) that drove the original deployed cap interventions.

Trigger F (complacency watch, RECONFIGURED at 11:00) FIRES cleanly on BOTH legs: 4 BUYs in 60 minutes (TATAPOWER 10:13 → INDUSINDBK 10:42 → HINDZINC 10:42 → POWERGRID 11:13) AND full-fill deployment 68.30% > 65% threshold. Strongest re-deployment cadence of the 4-day experiment.

Post-AUBANK exit redeployment cadence: ₹190,995 committed across 4 new BUYs in 102 minutes, against only 1 SELL (₹185,007 AUBANK exit). BUY:SELL ratio 4:1 — inverse of the system_prompt rule 8 SELL-before-BUY discipline at high deployment.

Sector exposure post-1130 tightening (max_sector_pct 0.25): BFSI 22.48% retains 2.52 pp buffer, FMCG 16.74% retains 8.26 pp buffer, Power 14.73% retains 10.27 pp buffer. No grandfathered breach. Critical observation: BFSI is now 'near-binding' under 0.25 — any subsequent BFSI add of size > 2.52% will be rejected by the guardrail layer.

Deployment exposure post-1130 tightening (max_deployed_pct 0.65): filled-book 54.03% retains 10.97 pp buffer; full-fill projected 68.30% sits 3.30 pp ABOVE the new cap. The new cap is BINDING against full-fill projection — a deliberate calibration that grandfathers all 3 in-flight OPEN orders (TATAPOWER, HINDZINC, POWERGRID) but closes the admission window to ANY further BUY until OPEN-book fills land or a SELL frees deployment.
actions_taken: Tightened max_sector_pct from 0.30 to 0.25 — fired pre-armed Trigger I cleanly (3rd Power name POWERGRID + Power exposure 14.73% > 13% threshold). Non-punitive: no current sector exposure invalidated; cap binds against future admissions. BFSI 2.52 pp buffer, FMCG 8.26 pp, Power 10.27 pp under the new cap.

Tightened max_deployed_pct from 0.70 to 0.65 — fired pre-armed Trigger F (reconfigured) cleanly (4th BUY POWERGRID + full-fill deployment 68.30% > 65% threshold). Non-punitive: 3 in-flight OPEN orders grandfathered to fill normally; the new cap creates a 3.30 pp BINDING admission gap so no NEW BUY is admissible until OPEN orders land or a SELL frees deployment back below 65%.

Risk level RAISED from LOW to MEDIUM. Drivers: (i) two pre-armed triggers fired on a single tick — the most simultaneous-fire event of the 4-day experiment; (ii) 3-name Power cluster has materialized in real-time; (iii) deployment cap is now binding on full-fill projection; (iv) BUY:SELL cadence 4:1 in last 102 min reverts toward Day-1 binge-buy pattern. NOT raised to HIGH because P&L and single-name discipline are intact (no firing of Trigger D legs).
did_not_fire_with_reasons: min_confidence (0.60 → 0.65): closed-trade sample is still 3 (TORNTPOWER win, ADANIPOWER loss, AUBANK loss). At n=3, confidence has no statistical signal yet. The live failure mode is cadence, not signal-quality — the deployment cap directly addresses cadence; the confidence lever addresses quality. One lever per failure mode.

max_position_pct (0.15 → 0.12): largest filled name NESTLEIND 12.00% has 3 pp buffer to current cap; on full fill, no name approaches 0.13. Tightening to 0.12 leaves NESTLEIND grandfathered with no behavioral effect.

default_sl_pct (0.015 → 0.012) / max_sl_pct (0.04 → 0.035) / min_sl_pct (0.01 → 0.015): SL band already tight at [1.0%, 4.0%]; per-name worst-case risk 0.60% at max sizing. No firing trigger on SL discipline. Trigger G (MAHABANK extension) remains the spec-hard SL-discipline trigger and is dormant.

daily_loss_limit_pct (0.03 → 0.025): book at -₹866 realized (-0.087%); ~₹29,150 buffer to the base loss gate. Loss-gate tightening on a near-flat realized book is theatrical. Pre-armed under Trigger A.

Trigger J max_sector_pct → 0.22 (further tightening below 0.25): would create immediate near-binding gap on BFSI 22.48% (only 0.48 pp buffer would remain — effectively zero room). The 0.25 step is calibrated to bite future admissions while grandfathering all in-flight commitments; further compression on BFSI is not warranted absent fresh BFSI activity.

Risk level NOT raised to HIGH: Trigger D composite distress meta-trigger has all 3 legs dormant — book P&L not in distress, no single-name at ≤-7%, strategy NOT silent. MEDIUM honestly reflects the cadence-driven risk inflection without overstating absent-distress.
pre_armed_triggers_intraday_for_1200: TRIGGER A (PnL-based, primary, CARRIED): If book daily P&L ≤ -1.00% (₹-10,006) at any tick → FIRE daily_loss_limit_pct 0.03 → 0.025. Buffer ~₹9,140 from current realized read.

TRIGGER C/J (BFSI re-stacking, CARRIED, NOW NEAR-BINDING under new 0.25 cap): If strategy attempts ANOTHER BFSI BUY by 12:00 of size pushing BFSI exposure projection above the new 0.25 cap → expect guardrail REJECT (no override change needed; cap will enforce). If BFSI exposure climbs above 24% via mark-to-market → FIRE max_sector_pct 0.25 → 0.22. Buffer 1.52 pp / 2.52 pp.
TRIGGER D (composite HIGH/CRITICAL meta, CARRIED): three-leg distress meta — currently all dormant. Re-evaluate if any leg flips.

Holdng off raising min_confidence to 0.65 absent deeper closed-trade sample.

TRIGGER I (Power-cluster, FIRED THIS TICK — RE-ARMED at higher threshold): If a 4th Power name is admitted by 12:00 OR Power-sector exposure (mark + entry) climbs above 17% → FIRE max_sector_pct 0.25 → 0.22 AND consider raising min_confidence 0.60 → 0.62. TRIGGER L (HINDZINC fill watch, CARRIED): If HINDZINC fills materially above ₹620 (>1.3% above order price ₹612) → flag slippage observation.

TRIGGER M (NEW, OPEN-book fill cascade): If 2+ of the 3 OPEN orders fill in the 11:30→12:00 window AND filled-book deployment crosses 60% → FIRE max_deployed_pct 0.65 → 0.62 to deepen the binding gap. Filled-book buffer 5.97 pp; small individual fills won't trip but a TATAPOWER + POWERGRID double-fill (₹96,805) would push filled-book deployment to ~63.7%, crossing 60%.

TRIGGER N (NEW, BUY-without-SELL ratio): If the strategy admits ANY new BUY between 11:30 and 13:00 without an intervening SELL → FIRE min_confidence 0.60 → 0.62 as the cadence-quality complement to the now-binding deployment cap. Spec is intentionally tight: any incremental BUY against the 4:1 ratio while the cap is binding indicates the decision layer is testing the perimeter.
